summary: Matched a substantial first slice of melee/mp/mpcoll.c, raising the file report to 20.63% matched over 46.32 KB. The PR added C coverage for many early mpColl routines, including fully matched ECB/collision setup helpers such as mpColl_80041C78, mpColl_80041C8C, mpColl_80041DD0, mpColl_80041EE4, mpColl_80042078, mpColl_8004220C, mpColl_80042384, mpColl_800424DC, mpColl_8004293C, mpColl_80042C58, mpColl_80042D24, mpColl_80042DB0, mpColl_80043268, mpColl_80043324, mpColl_80043558, mpColl_80043754, mpColl_800439FC, mpColl_80043ADC, mpColl_80043BBC, mpColl_8004730C, and mpColl_800477E0. It also added a large partial implementation of mpColl_80046904, refined mp collision/fighter headers, introduced a callback typedef, and renamed CollData::x138 to prev_env_flags. There were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added includes for ftCommon/forward.h and mp/forward.h to mpcoll.c and mpcoll.h to support newly typed collision box and callback declarations.
Declared mpColl_804D64A0, mpColl_804D64A4, and mpColl_804D64A8 as static Event values in mpcoll.c and declared mpColl_804D64AC as a u32 global.
Converted numerous local variables, constants, helpers, and prototypes from f32 to float, apparently to satisfy matching and signature consistency in this file.
Changed mpColl_80042C58 and mpColl_8004730C from raw float-array/ftECB-style parameters to ftCollisionBox* parameters, with field access changed to top, bottom, right, and left members.
Added mpColl_Callback in src/melee/mp/forward.h as bool (*)(CollData*, u32), and changed mpColl_80043754 to take that callback type and return bool.
Renamed CollData field x138 to prev_env_flags in src/melee/ft/types.h and updated mpcoll.c uses such as mpColl_800477E0 and mpColl_8004730C.

Added or exposed prototypes in mpcoll.h for more mpColl routines, including mpColl_80044E10, mpColl_800454A4, mpColl_80045B74, mpColl_80046224, mpColl_80046904, mpColl_8004C864, mpColl_8004C91C, mpColl_800528CC, and mpColl_80052A98.
Adjusted mplib.h prototypes to use float spelling and add/refine argument names for collision helper functions such as mpLib_800501CC, mpLib_800509B8, and mpLib_80051EC8.
Updated ftNs_AttackHi4_YoyoCheckEnvColl to use named CollData coll and ftCollisionBox ecb locals instead of anonymous stack arrays, then call the new ftCollisionBox-based collision path.

Added local collision flag defines near mpColl_80046904, including Collide_LeftWallPush, Collide_RightWallMask, Collide_CeilingPush, Collide_FloorHug, ledge grab/slip flags, and CollisionFlagAir_* values.
Implemented mpColl_80046904 as a large air-collision callback with named comments for wall, ceiling, floor, squeeze, and ledge-grab phases, though the report shows it was still only 38.84% matched.

Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt with many mpColl scratch statuses, including done__func_800424DC, done__func_8004293C, done__func_80042C58, done__func_80042D24, done__func_80042DB0, done__func_80043268, done__func_80043324, done__func_80043754, done__func_800439FC, done__func_80043BBC, and mpColl_800477E0.
Regenerated or heavily rewrote asm/melee/mp/mpcoll.s address annotations and labels from placeholder offsets to real 0x80041C78-range addresses while source coverage changed.
smart_moves: Introduced mpColl_Callback instead of repeatedly spelling an untyped function pointer; this made mpColl_80043754 and mpColl_80046904 easier to connect and reuse.
Renamed CollData::x138 to prev_env_flags based on observed behavior where collision entry points save env_flags before clearing it.
Replaced opaque ftNs_AttackHi4 stack float-array usage with ftCollisionBox, using named ecb.top, ecb.bottom, ecb.right, and ecb.left fields; this helped one caller align with the mpcoll API.

Added descriptive temporary names and inline comments in the large mpColl_80046904 partial, such as Physics_LeftWallCheckAir, Physics_SqueezeHorizontal, Collision_GetNextNonCeilingLine, and Physics_CheckForRightLedge, preserving reverse-engineering knowledge even before full matching.
Kept collision flag macros local to the newly worked area and included a TODO noting they duplicate existing MPCOLL_* naming, avoiding premature global naming churn while still documenting semantics.
Used small inline helper functions such as clamp_above, clamp_below, update_min_max, Vec2_Interpolate, and max_inline to express repeated assembly patterns cleanly.
Tracked decomp.me scratch provenance in comments and scratches.txt, which makes later work auditable and searchable.
decomp_lessons: For collision systems, turning anonymous float arrays into named geometric structs can unlock both matching and cross-file readability; ftCollisionBox clarified mpcoll and Ness yoyo collision code.
When a field is repeatedly assigned from env_flags immediately before env_flags is reset, prev_env_flags is a strong evidence-based name.
Callback signatures should be promoted into forward headers once a dispatcher/callback pair is identified; mpColl_Callback captured the mpColl_80043754 to mpColl_80046904 relationship.

Large collision functions can be progressed safely by naming intermediate phases and flags even when the function remains partial; mpColl_80046904 documents wall, ceiling, floor, squeeze, platform-pass, and ledge-grab behaviors.
Float spelling can matter for matching and consistency; this PR systematically preferred float over f32 in touched prototypes and locals.
Local defines with TODOs are acceptable as a staging tactic when semantic flag names are known but final project-wide naming conventions are unresolved.
Caller cleanups may be necessary to land a callee signature improvement; ftNs_AttackHi4_YoyoCheckEnvColl was updated after mpColl_8004730C moved to ftCollisionBox*.
naming_conventions: mp collision functions continue to use address-based names in the form mpColl_800xxxxx when no final semantic name is established.
New callback typedef uses subsystem prefix and role: mpColl_Callback.
CollData::x138 was renamed to prev_env_flags to describe saved previous environment flags.
ftCollisionBox fields are used semantically as top, bottom, right, and left instead of raw float-array indexing.
Local collision flag names used Collide_* and CollisionFlagAir_* prefixes, but the code contains a TODO that these duplicate MPCOLL_RIGHTWALL-style names and need a final convention.
Temporary variables in mpColl_80046904 use descriptive collision-phase names such as horizontal_squeeze_flags, touched_floor, x_after_collide_left, y_after_collide_ceiling, and not_touched_floor.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched many small and medium ECB/collision helper functions first, building enough typed scaffolding to support larger mpcoll work.
Used inline helper functions to reproduce compare-and-clamp/min-max codegen patterns.
Used a named callback typedef and bool return types to align control-flow expectations between mpColl_80043754 and collision callbacks.
Used typed ftCollisionBox access to replace stack float arrays and improve both codegen and semantic confidence.
Preserved or added decomp.me scratch URLs/statuses as matching evidence and future search anchors.
Applied float-order hacks/constants such as mpColl_804D7F9C, mpColl_804D7FA0, flt_804D7FD8, flt_804D7FF8, and flt_804D8010 where ordering or literal placement mattered.
The asm diff shows labels and comments moved from placeholder offsets like .L_00000124 to absolute labels like .L_80041D9C, useful when reconciling remaining assembly against newly compiled C.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The only explicit naming guidance in the diff is an author TODO near collision flag defines: the new Collide_* names duplicate existing MPCOLL_RIGHTWALL-style constants and a convention should be chosen later.
follow_up_queries: Search for later PRs that resolve the TODO choosing between Collide_* and MPCOLL_* collision flag naming.
Search for later matches of mpColl_80046904 beyond 38.84%.
Search for additional uses or refinements of CollData::prev_env_flags.
Search for later ftCollisionBox/ftECB type cleanups in fighter collision callers.
Search for final semantic names for mpColl_80044E10, mpColl_800454A4, mpColl_80045B74, mpColl_80046224, mpColl_8004C864, and mpColl_8004C91C.
